import re

with open\('app.css', 'r', encoding='utf-8'\) as f:
    content = f.read\(\)

# Find all rules for the selectors mentioned
selectors = ['topnav', 'topbar', 'navbar-custom', 'ebank-btn', 'btn-enajava', 
             'btn-eregistration', 'win-icon', 'home-what-i-need', 'topbar-right-menu']

# Pattern to match CSS rules: selector { content }
pattern = r'\\.\(' + '|'.join\(s.replace\('-', r'\\-'\) for s in selectors\) + r'\)[^{]*\\{[^}]*\\}'

matches = re.findall\(pattern, content\)

for selector in selectors:
    # More specific pattern for each selector
    sel_pattern = r'\\.' + selector.replace\('-', r'\\-'\) + r'[^\\{]*\\{[^}]*\\}'
    rules = re.findall\(sel_pattern, content, re.DOTALL\)
    if rules:
        print\(f"\\n=== {selector} ==="\)
        for rule in rules[:5]:  # First 5 matches
            print\(rule[:200]\)
